title: helianthemum glaucum subsp. croceum
intro: Helianthemum glaucum subsp. croceum, also known as Rockrose, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its pinkish-yellow flowers and silvery-gray foliage. Traditionally, Rockrose has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to its high content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Rockrose are thought to include soothing digestive issues, reducing stress and anxiety, and promoting wound healing due to its antimicrobial properties. Rockrose can be prepared into herbal remedies such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically for skin issues and internally for digestive issues.
